perturbation.original_text: The COMPANY will give stock to the CELEBRITY which will be restricted for one year from issuance and will be as follows: Monthly Sales Volume Stock Options -------------------- ------------- $ 300,000 300,000 shares 600,000 600,000 shares 1,000,000 1,000,000 shares These stock options will remain in place until certificates are issued once volumes are met as long as the CELEBRITY maintains the distributorship by paying the annual dues of $15.00.
perturbation.changed_text: The COMPANY will give stock to the CELEBRITY which will be restricted for one year from issuance and will be as follows: Monthly Sales Volume Stock Options -------------------- ------------- $ 300,000 300,000 shares 600,000 600,000 shares 1,000,000 1,000,000 shares These stock options will be immediately vested and certificates will be issued at the end of the agreement, regardless of sales volume and CELEBRITY annual membership fee.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the vesting and issuance of stock options. Originally, the vesting and issuance of certificates were contingent on meeting monthly sales volume targets and maintaining the distributorship. The modified text states that the stock options will be immediately vested and certificates will be issued at the end of the agreement, regardless of sales volume. This contradicts the original terms and creates uncertainty about how the stock options will be handled, violating the original financial terms of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CELEBRITY will receive $6,000 per month for entire duration of the agreement. The COMPANY will give stock to the CELEBRITY which will be restricted for one year from issuance and will be as follows: Monthly Sales Volume Stock Options -------------------- ------------- $ 300,000 300,000 shares 600,000 600,000 shares 1,000,000 1,000,000 shares These stock options will be immediately vested and certificates will be issued at the end of the agreement, regardless of sales volume and CELEBRITY annual membership fee.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by altering the conditions under which the CELEBRITY receives stock options. Originally, stock options were contingent on meeting monthly sales volumes. The changed text states that stock options are immediately vested and certificates will be issued regardless of sales volume and CELEBRITY annual membership fee, directly conflicting with the earlier sales volume requirements presented in both the original and contradicted sections. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the conditions for vesting, impacting the financial terms of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement on the date above written. The COMPANY CELEBRITY The Right Solution Bruce Jenner A Nevada Corporation Independent Contractor/COMPANY By ____________________________ By _________________________________ Rick Bailey President / CEO
perturbation.changed_text: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement on the date above written. The COMPANY CELEBRITY The Right Solution Bruce Jenner A Nevada Corporation Employee/COMPANY By ____________________________ By _________________________________ Rick Bailey President / CEO
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces contradiction because the contract in section 5 defines the celebrity as an independent contractor while in the signature they are described as Employee. Now it contradicts the terms of the contract on what is the agreement between the company and celebrity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR CELEBRITY and CELEBRITY Personnel will act as an independent contractor in the performance of its duties under this Agreement. Accordingly, CELEBRITY will be responsible for payment of all federal, state, and local taxes on compensation paid under this Agreement, including income and social security taxes, unemployment insurance, and any other taxes due relative to Distributor's Personnel and any and all business license fees as may be required. This Agreement neither expressly nor impliedly creates a --- relationship of principal and agent, or employee and employer, between Distributor's Personnel and the COMPANY. Neither CELEBRITY nor CELEBRITY Personnel are authorized to enter into any agreements on behalf of the COMPANY. The COMPANY expressly retains the right to approve, in its sole discretion, each Asset Opportunity or Business Opportunity introduced by CELEBRITY, and to make all final decisions with respect to effecting a transaction on any Business Opportunity.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Independent Contractor/COMPANY' to 'Employee/COMPANY' directly contradicts the 'INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR CELEBRITY' clause which explicitly states that the celebrity acts as an independent contractor and is responsible for their own taxes, and that the agreement does not create an employer-employee relationship. This change introduces a fundamental conflict regarding the nature of the relationship between the company and the celebrity, making the contract internally inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.
